column1: # Cross-model tolerance overrides for Layer 4 of the statistics-drift harness
# (Issue #224 Decision 9). The harness pairs *-default and *-bin-data-model
# scenarios row-by-row and reports per-cell deviation against the tier ladder
# below. Overrides in this file widen the T2/T3 thresholds for a specific
# (scenario, column) pair, a single column across all scenarios, or all
# columns of a single scenario. Missing rows → default ladder applies.
# Default ladder (from Decision 9):
#   T1: bitwise identical                          advisory
#   T2: deviation ≤ 1%                             advisory
#   T3: deviation ≤ 4%                             advisory (per #287)
#   T4: deviation > 4%                             BLOCKING
# Resolution rules (from Decision 9):
#   - non-blank scenario + non-blank column → applies only to that pair
#   - blank scenario + non-blank column     → applies to that column across all scenarios
#   - non-blank scenario + blank column     → applies to all columns of that scenario
#   - both blank                            → syntax error; engine refuses to start
#   - multiple matches                      → most-specific wins (scenario+column > column-only > scenario-only)
# Empirical baseline for #287 (per Commit 2 + Commit 6 measurement against
# the four canonical datasets in scenarios.tsv): bin-path percentile
# deviation from raw fits within the default 4% T3 envelope on all canonical
# datasets at bpd=53. Bin-resolution bound per #187 R4 is ~2.2% bin width
# (~1.1% midpoint error) plus tail-percentile effects from sparse bin
# population — observed worst case 3.81% on tomcat-bin-data-model p9999.
# Exact-value statistics (min, mean, max, std_dev, cv, skewness, kurtosis,
# bimodality_coef) are derived from Welford-Pébay sidecars and produce
# values mathematically equal to the raw path modulo floating-point order-
# of-operations. Observed worst case is ~1e-13 relative deviation — well
# within T1 (bitwise within float precision). No overrides needed.
# This file ships with no overrides — the default ladder is sufficient for
# the canonical datasets. Empirical L4 measurements that exceed the
# default ladder add rows here, reviewed via TSV diff.
# Schema:
scenario
